acceptanceCriteria.criterion: A legitimate generated lockfile is validated, committed and published by one bounded repair turn
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: The coordinator provides exact paths, a stable fingerprint and redacted prior-command context to a single repair role, then requires clean remote-confirmed HEAD.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: An unrelated or unsafe tracked change blocks with a precise diagnosis
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Unsafe changes remain untouched and a single coordinator-owned escalation records fingerprint, paths and porcelain status.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: The same dirty-tree fingerprint cannot consume repeated generic retries
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Only one dirty repair is admitted per delivery cycle; a remaining or subsequent dirty candidate is escalated instead of reopening implementation.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Evidence and reviews restart only at the new remote-confirmed HEAD
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: CandidateHead confirms clean local/remote identity after repair and the review cycle restarts from that exact SHA before finalization.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Complete Symphony tests and recovery gates pass
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: 335 tests passed with 100% coverage; format, specs, Credo and Dialyzer passed on the exact published commit.

summary: Dirty candidates now enter one safe, diagnosed repair turn and cannot bypass exact-HEAD review durability or loop through generic implementation retries.
